hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 complementarity, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve selectivity by avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is updated to emphasize the importance of targeted mutagenesis at non-pharmacophore positions to enhance SSTR2 selectivity. Strong ddG alone is insufficient; the design must actively disrupt off-target interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to form conserved contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on them allows for targeted disruption of off-target interactions while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
These mutations are designed to break conserved hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, while preserving the pharmacophore core (FWKT at 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
